architectural_patterns.justification: The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. The explanation matches the client-server model described in section 9.1, where clients (users) make requests to a server (software processing requests).
The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 9.1 describes the three-tier architecture: Presentation Layer, Business Logic Layer, and Data Layer.

The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 describes multiple microservices (Api Gateway, Core, Analytics Module, Api Player, Web Socket) interacting with each other.

The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 explains that the API Gateway handles all client requests, manages authentication, authorization, SSL, and routing to internal microservices.
The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes the use of AWS services like S3 for storing structured and unstructured data at scale.

The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 explains that data transmission from producers (microservices) to consumers (storage/analysis) is handled via streaming services (Kinesis Data Streams).

components.justification: The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 describes the web and mobile interfaces communicating via HTTP protocol.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 describes the API Gateway's responsibilities, including managing, authenticating, and authorizing users.
The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 describes the Core service as the main business logic of the platform.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 describes the Analytics Module's function of obtaining and processing data from playlogs, connections, and platform actions.
The component name appears to be misspelled ('Api weird' instead of 'Api Player'). The role and technical details are partially correct but lack clarity. The communication description is incomplete.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.2 describes the Web Socket's role in enabling bidirectional communication between the platform and players.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes the MySQL database's role in storing necessary data for system functioning.
The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes Amazon S3 as part of the Data Lake for storing historical documents.
The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes Amazon DynamoDB as a non-relational database for real-time storage.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes Kinesis Data Streams as a data ingestion and processing service for streaming data.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes Kinesis Data Firehose as a service for loading large amounts of data into data stores.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes Amazon Athena as a serverless query service for querying data stored in S3.

The role, technical details, and communication are all correctly explained and supported by the document. Section 9.2.3 describes AWS Glue as a serverless data integration service used for metadata storage.

design_patterns.justification: The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 10.2 explains that NestJS Providers (Services) are designed to be injected as dependencies into Modules or other components.
The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 10.2 explains that Classes responsible for receiving HTTP requests and processing them are Controllers.
The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 10.2 explains that Classes containing the business logic of the application are Services.
The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 11.2.1 explains that the @Cron decorator from @nestjs/schedule is used to execute functions periodically.

The pattern is correctly identified and supported by the document. Section 10.2 explains that @UseGuards decorator is used to perform validation and permission checks before accessing endpoint functions.
